title: Official 30 title You Can Not Redo
display_title: Official 30 title You Can Not Redo
wikitext:
[]: Official 3.0 title: You Can (Not) Redo             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sat Dec 31, 2011 11:23 am So it's been finally revealed! What do you guys think it means? Better hope it's not a reference to the sequel theory, heheh.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sat Dec 31, 2011 11:38 am It means we've got 3 threads pertaining to this already and the mods will probably tiy them all up.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sat Dec 31, 2011 11:42 am Source: Khara updated it right when the clock struck midnight on Jan 1st 2012. http://www.evangelion.co.jp/index.html

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Dec 31, 2011 12:10 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 31, 2011 12:11 pm This new pics that people are referring to as "Neko-Asuka" seems to be the helmet she removes in the trailer. Compare/contrast Mari's helmet. Elfen Lied has given me a fetish for hair spilling out from beneath a helmet, so go helmet. Also, yeah, finally news. seems like they will finish before the actual year 2015 XD "You can (not) redo", heh? Im happy that there's one of these titles at all, even if I would have expected them to pick another auxilary verb. The title seems to suggest that the story will deviate a lot from the original, which means completely new stuff yeah. It might refer to the process of "remaking" (And perhaps some realization of Anno's that he could not make it a remake now that ten years have passed and he's become a different person.) It might refer to how the situation will be doubtlessly changed by the near apocalypse, and how Shinji will have to live with the consequences of the choise he made, how he can't go back to being the nonthreatening, ignorant kid after he almost ripped a hole into the fabric of reality. Still hoping for "You will not survive" for part 4. The "Final"-related news were something that I had not expected, given the general lack of news, but... yeah, let's see if they stick to it. If everything goes well, we might see trailers by this summer. This also means seperate movies, which is also great, first 'cause we all like to have as much awesome eva material as possible, and second because the plot and the characters really deserve the time. They have to properly introduce Mari and Kaworu in Q, after all...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 31, 2011 12:12 pm In the context of this new continiuty, probably how Shinji can't go back and fix screw-ups, instead he has to go forward with life: Asuka and 9th Angel, saving Rei from the 10th Angel and almost causing Third Impact, and whatever the hell will happen in 3.0 --maybe this would include killing Kaworu but who knows if Angel-boy would stick around until Final or not. I feel like there's a particular series line either from EoTV or EoE that's quite applicable here, but I'm blanking out on it. (I guess Misato's farewell speech in EoE is fitting.) Although, Misato's line during Shinji's Episode 20 headtrip is fitting too: Episode 20 wrote: Shinji: Where am I? Shinji: Inside the Eva. Shinji: Inside the Eva? Did I pilot the Eva again? Why? Misato: You won't pilot the Eva anymore? Shinji: I've decided I will never pilot Eva again. Misato: But you did pilot Evangelion Unit 01. Shinji, you are here now because you piloted the Eva. You are the person you are now because you piloted the Eva. You cannot deny it, that you in fact piloted the Eva, nor can you deny the self that you have been so far, which is your past. But as for what you will do from now on, you must decide for yourself. Shinji: I... I...

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sat Dec 31, 2011 12:39 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Edit: Could those with more immediate and thorough Japanese culture knowledge etc help maybe shed light on what is likely meant by 'Redo' here? Is it a 'do-over' notion as in 'reset and try again' like I'm thinking or is it sort of a 'do it the same a second time' or what? Is it computer program jargon? Honestly the word 'redo' has almost no common usage in English in any context I'm aware of other than computer program interfaces that allow for 'undo'.

[]:             Quote Post by Xous » Sat Dec 31, 2011 12:52 pm When I first saw the title You Can (Not) Redo the first thing that came to mind was the word "rewind," so I agree with the notion that "Redo" could be referring to Shinji being unable to go back to the days where he didn't try to destroy the world... It'd be funny if Shinji wakes up from piloting Unit-01 after Near Third Impact, sees the destruction and yells, "I WANT A REDO!" Seeing the title didn't make me think of the Sequel Theory at all, though I can see how it can be interpreted that way as well. Only time will tell, I guess.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 31, 2011 1:12 pm Xous wrote: When I first saw the title You Can (Not) Redo the first thing that came to mind was the word "rewind,"

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Sat Dec 31, 2011 1:15 pm Cycle, not sequel :P

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Dec 31, 2011 1:36 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Dec 31, 2011 1:49 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: You know what would've been a better title? You Can (Not) Rebuild ... See, just like you guys said I'm already picking this movie apart. But, no, seriously, that'd be a pretty good title considering the destruction and broken connections from at the end of 2.0 all around. Full of lush dual meaning and applications, from the city to individual lives and bodies to specific relationships...

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Dec 31, 2011 2:04 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: That can be the 4.0/Final titles. You may no be able to redo/undo, but you can always rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Sat Dec 31, 2011 2:13 pm Wouldn't reset be a better word? Oh well, at least it is something, although I'm more interested in the names of Kushiel and Othniel at this point.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sat Dec 31, 2011 2:31 pm Welp, this sure does fit in perfectly with my theory of WTF the Rebuild movies are up to thematically.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sat Dec 31, 2011 3:01 pm Xous wrote: When I first saw the title You Can (Not) Redo the first thing that came to mind was the word "rewind," so I agree with the notion that "Redo" could be referring to Shinji being unable to go back to the days where he didn't try to destroy the world... It'd be funny if Shinji wakes up from piloting Unit-01 after Near Third Impact, sees the destruction and yells, "I WANT A REDO!" Seeing the title didn't make me think of the Sequel Theory at all, though I can see how it can be interpreted that way as well. Only time will tell, I guess.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 31, 2011 3:22 pm The thing with the sequel theory is that physical cues in the setting as depicted don't really mean much, since a reset world can look like pretty much anything; sequel or no, pretty much all bets are off either way. I'm somewhat warming to the idea of the NME being an iteration of Instrumentality, where Shinji's attempting to do it over again in hopes of "getting it right". But, as he's discovering at the end of YC(n)A, life doesn't give you do-overs. Maybe Anno's come to accept this as well. Perfect version of Eva? There isn't one. Eva is Eva, and it can't be anything other than what it is. The new movies may or may not add to it, but they will not supplant it. And that's okay. Part of me hopes he'll tie everything together to hammer that point home. Part of me hopes it really is a separate continuity, and will stand or fall on its own. Even now I still wouldn't bet money on it either way (and truthfully I still don't really care, content as I am with what I have). But the title is intriguing regardless, and makes me think finding out what happens next is gonna be one hell of a ride.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Dec 31, 2011 3:27 pm As a fan of the theory myself, the "Redo" title is much too ambiguous. As some have speculated, it could be a downright rebuttal to the sequel theory (Though, I'm not sure how popular it is in Japan or if Khara is aware of it at all), it could be something to do with it, or it could just be totally unrelated. Bagheera wrote: But the title is intriguing regardless, and makes me think finding out what happens next is gonna be one hell of a ride.

[]:             Quote Post by Xous » Sat Dec 31, 2011 4:40 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I still think this shot down any hopes for Sequel Theory, and for various reasons--a sequel wouldn't make sense. I prefer NME's approach of nods/"what if-s" to the original series, anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Dec 31, 2011 4:44 pm Finally we get a title, this kind of reenforces the notion that 3.0 is going to be ultra depressing. Still the wait is killing me, hope that Shinji faces the consequences of saving Rei. *mental breakdown pending*
